plang.note: Includes the new BuilderSanity smoke test (passes). 6 remaining failures are `*.fixture.goal` files designed to fail — backing fixtures for test.report tests. Test discovery picks them up despite their intentional-failure role.

builder_validation.result: all 4 goals built successfully; BuilderSanity.test.goal then ran via `plang --test` and PASSED. Builder is functional.

findings.issue: 6 `*.fixture.goal` files report as failing in `plang --test`. They are intentional-failure fixtures backing TestReportMasksSensitiveVariables / TestReportRendersFailureWithVariables. Discovery picks them up as standalone tests because `.fixture.goal` extension isn't excluded.
findings.impact: Suite reports 6 red tests that are 'expected' to fail. Either the convention (leading underscore folder + `.fixture` extension) should exclude them from discovery, or the fixtures should live outside Tests/. Right now they pollute the pass/fail count and force tester verdicts to FAIL on every run.
findings.suggestion: Coder: update test discovery to skip files matching `*.fixture.goal` OR folders prefixed with `_`. Then re-run — should be 212/212 green.

summary: v3 PASS on substance — coder's commits (path canonical form, builder teaching for condition.if, .pr rebuild) closed every behavioral PLang failure from v2. C# 3036/3036 stays green. The new BuilderSanity smoke test (added to repo at Tests/BuilderSanity/) confirms the builder is functional. Strict-red rule applies: 6 `*.fixture.goal` files report as failing because test discovery includes them — these are intentional-failure fixtures, but until discovery excludes them or they get explicit out-of-scope acceptance, the verdict is FAIL. Going from 21 red to 6 red is excellent progress; the remaining 6 are a discovery-config issue, not a code bug.
